honest-gherkin/src/honest_gherkin/ir.py:: TypedDict total= is a static-only annotation; at runtime total=True and total=False produce an identical class whose instances are plain dicts with no required-key enforcement. parse builds every Step with all four keys, so no constructible input distinguishes the two.
honest-gherkin/src/honest_gherkin/cli.py: dest="command"->"" stores the chosen subcommand under the "" attribute; main reads only args.path and args.steps, never args.command, so no behaviour changes.
prog="honest-gherkin"->"" affects only argparse usage/help/error text; main reads no prog and the exit codes and reports are unchanged.
the emptied string is the subparser help text (the "run" name itself is caught); help affects only --help output, not parsing or exit codes.
the emptied string is the argument help text (the "path" dest is caught); help affects only --help output.
the emptied string is the argument help text (the "--steps" flag is caught); help affects only --help output.
if __name__ == "" is never true under import (name is honest_gherkin.cli) nor as a script (name is __main__), so the guarded block never runs; unreachable under test, equivalent.
removes the __main__ guard block, which only runs when cli.py is executed as a script; under import the block never runs, so behaviour is identical.
replaces the __main__ guard body with pass; the guard is False under import so neither the original raise nor pass ever executes — equivalent.
